title: hieracium vogesiacum subsp. angliciforme
intro: Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. angliciforme, also known as the hawkweed or devil's paintbrush, is a plant species within the dandelion family known for its distinctive yellow or orange flowers and medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
